entry.title: Re: End to End Guide: Enabling SAP Joule in SAP S/4HANA Fiori Launchpad (PCE + OnPremise PoC)
entry.content:
Yes @AlonsoF , on-premise S/4HANA works for a basic read-only co-pilot scenario on BTP Public Edition.
You connect via SAP Cloud Connector, expose OData services through SAP Gateway, and the AI agent on BTP reads data through BTP Destinations. Joule can also be embedded in your Fiori Launchpad using IAS with SSO. No S/4HANA Cloud is strictly required for this foundation.

However, for full capabilities we recommend S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, and here is why:
  • OData coverage — Private Cloud ships with significantly more pre-activated, stable, and versioned OData APIs out of the box. With on premises, many services need to be manually registered (also many not available) in /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE, and not all DPC methods are implemented (we ran into several 501 Not Implemented responses on standard ISU/FICA services)
  • BTP Formation — S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition integrates natively with BTP via Formations, which auto-provisions destinations, service bindings, and connectivity configuration. On premises does not support Formations on BTP, so everything must be wired manually or workarounds has to be implemented.
Short answer:
  • On premises = possible, good for a PoC, but expect gaps in API availability and manual workarounds
  • S/4HANA Cloud Private = recommended with full BTP integration
Happy to elaborate on any specific integration point!

entry.title: Re: Attachments uploaded from SSAM with Arabic description is updated as ######## in backend syste
entry.content: If the client URL-encodes the slug but ENCODE_ASCII is off, users will see %xx sequences in filename/description. If the client sends raw text and ENCODE_ASCII is on, unescape_url only helps if the text was actually escaped; otherwise there is no meaningful conversion benefit. Could you please verify on the gateway traces how the discription is being posted in the request and how the response looks? The patch is already applied to my understanding and the data filter has correct value, correct?

entry.title: Input help has landed in Define Processing Rules
entry.content:
A small note alongside this week's What's New in SAP Logistics Management, on something that didn't make it into the headline list but is worth a few minutes of your attention if you spend time in Define Processing Rules.

For a while now, several of you have been telling us — through tickets, partner sessions, and the occasional hallway conversation — that authoring a business rule meant knowing your data by heart. The product ID. The packing work center ID. The print queue name. Every field a blank text box, every typo discovered later, every lookup an alt-tab to another app.

We heard you. As of this week, those fields offer input help.

 

What that looks like

Open the rule editor. Click into a condition or output value field that references master data, and instead of a blank text box you get a dropdown of the values your tenant actually has, with search-as-you-type.

The dropdown is context-aware, so as you fill in earlier fields the choices for later ones narrow to what makes sense in your scenario.

The same input help shows up in the rule testing dialog, so trying out a rule no longer means hunting for valid input parameters in another tab.

And if you do manage to enter a value that no longer exists — a `OUTB_STANDRD` where you meant `OUTB_STANDARD` —, the system stops the save and tells you what's wrong, rather than letting a broken rule sit there waiting to fail at runtime.

It isn't a revolutionary change. But we believe it'll save real time for the people who configure these rules every day, and we wanted to make sure it didn't go unnoticed.

 

 

To the customers and partners who raised this — including the very direct ones who told us, in so many words, "please give us a search help, this is painful" — thank you. Keep the feedback coming. It's the most useful thing you can send us.

 

Further Information

SAP Logistics Management | Product Page

SAP Logistics Management | Business Process Automation

entry.title: Extra Objects getting populated in the Calibration PM order
entry.content:
I need some urgent help analyzing a PM issue. I come from a PTP/MM background and have very limited knowledge of SAP PM, so I’m struggling to identify the root cause.

 

Issue:

 

When a Calibration PM Order is created from a Maintenance Plan where the Reference Object contains both a Functional Location and an Equipment, the generated PM Order contains additional objects.

 

Instead of only the Functional Location and Equipment maintained in the Maintenance Plan, the Objects tab in the PM Order also contains four additional Equipment records. These four Equipment always belong to the different Functional Location, which is unrelated to the Maintenance Plan. Regardless of which Functional Location and Equipment are maintained in the Maintenance Plan, these same four Equipment are always added to the order.

 

However, I noticed that for other Calibration PM Orders created from Maintenance Plans where only the Equipment is maintained in the Reference Object, the generated order behaves correctly. The Objects tab contains only the Equipment and its corresponding Functional Location, with no additional Equipment.

 

I’m trying to understand from where are these extra four Equipment being derived? Any guidance on how to analyze this would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: MIGO - PPV Not posting to CO-PA
entry.content:
Hello,

We have an issue where the MIGO PPV posting is not posting to COPA, yet the CO Document is created with the PSG Object. We managed to use KE4SMM to post to COPA, however this is not a suitable solution. 

The scenario - MTO - Requirement class - KEB where the Account Assignment category is 'M' i.e. w/o CO controlling. The PO -> MIGO has assigned the Sales Order, therefore COS is via the Billing - VPRS condition, but the PPV is required to post to CO-PA at MIGO as material is Standard priced.

I have looked at various SAP Notes and nothing seems relevant. The concern is that KE4SMM does post the PPV to CO-PA, however was expecting that MIGO would post to CO-PA via KEI2 / OKB9 settings for the PPV account. Also, MIGO creates the CO doument with PSG Object.

Any advice is much appreciated 

Kind regards,

Jeremy Morkel

entry.title: Guide to User Parameter Setup in SAP Transportation Management
entry.content:
What are user parameters in SAP ?

User parameters (also called SET/GET parameters or parameter IDs) are key–value pairs stored against an individual user master record. When a transaction reads a parameter ID, it picks up that user's value and adjusts its behaviour accordingly.This blog looks at how such parameters are applied in SAP TM. Across the system, the planning, cockpit, optimizer, charge calculation, and integration components

How to maintain user parameters ?

You can maintain these parameters in two common ways:

Option A – maintain your own parameters with SU3:

  1. Run transaction SU3 (Maintain Own User Defaults).
  2. Switch to the Parameters tab.
  3. In a new row, enter the Parameter ID (for example /SCMTMS/EXP ).
  4. Enter the Parameter Value. For on/off switches use X; for others enter the relevant ID, code or number described in the tables below.
  5. Save. The new behavior applies the next time the relevant transaction is opened.

Option B – maintain another user's parameters with SU01:

Administrators use SU01 to open a specific user, then set the same Parameter ID and Parameter Value on that user's Parameters tab. This is how you roll a standard set of defaults out to a planner role or a technical user.

Parameter reference by category

The tables below group the parameters by functional area. For each one you get a short description of what it does, guidance on when to use it, and a recommended setting.

1. UI and Display

Parameter ID

What it does / when to use it

Setting / Value

/SCMTMS/UI_DISPSETT

parameter allows the admin user to set the UI settings for multiple end users at once.

Refer KBA : 3477460

Set the relevant display-setting ID per role

 

2.Transportation Cockpit

The cockpit is where planners spend most of their day. These parameters control the cockpit entry experience, on-screen logging and how the system reacts to technical assertions.

Parameter ID

What it does / when to use it

Setting / Value

/SCMTMS/UI_PLN_LOG

Transportation Cockpit: Activate Log/Trace functionality. Useful when diagnosing cockpit performance or optimizer behaviour.

Set to 'X' temporarily for troubleshooting

/SCMTMS/TC_ENTRY_SCR

Shows the cockpit entry (selection) screen so the user can choose or vary the selection profile and planning profile before the cockpit opens.

Set to 'X' If the user want to display the Profile and Layout Set screen, in spite of the fact that system behaviour is customized that this screen should not be displayed

/SCMTMS/TC_DIS_DLVPR

Displays the delivery profile within the Transportation Cockpit context Refer KBA : 3159893 

Set to 'X'

/SCMTMS/TC_TECASSERT

Governs how the cockpit reacts to a technical assertion

0 = terminate processing

1 = warning message

2 or ' ' = do nothing, go on with normal processing

3.Planning

Planning parameters affect how the cockpit behaves around save, parallel access, manual assignment and inconsistencies.

Parameter ID

What it does / when to use it

Setting / Value

/SCMTMS/PLN_REFRESH

Refreshes the cockpit automatically after a save so the planner gets immediate visual confirmation of the result.

Enable for planners who need instant feedback. Set to 'X'

/SCMTMS/PLN_PARALLEL

Enables parallel planning in the cockpit on the same set of documents. parameter lets two users plan different stages of the same Freight Unit simultaneously Refer KBA 2518817 

Set to 'X'

/SCMTMS/PLN_MP_ASTR

Sets the manual planning assignment strategy (temporary setting). Drives how items are assigned across resources in multi-resource scenarios.

Set per planner role for multi-resource planning

/SCMTMS/PLN_INCONSIS

Controls inconsistency handling: 0 raises an assert, 1 reports the inconsistency.

Set to 'X' or ‘0’

4.Order Scheduling

These parameters define the default scheduling behavior and message for order scheduling

Parameter ID

What it does / when to use it

Setting / Value

/SCMTMS/OS_PLAN_PROF

Sets the default planning profile used for order scheduling.

Set planning profile  per business requirement

/SCMTMS/OS_DETAILED

Issues detailed messages during order scheduling, which helps support and key users diagnose why a schedule failed.

Set to 'X'

5.Freight Unit Building

Freight Unit Building Rules (FUBR) determine how freight units are formed. These parameters set the default rule and enable equipment-type maintenance.

Parameter ID

What it does / when to use it

Setting / Value

/SCMTMS/FUBR_DEF

Defines the default freight unit building rule for order scheduling, so the system knows which FUBR to apply when none is explicitly determined.

FUBR

/SCMTMS/FUBR_EQUI

Enables maintenance of the 'Equipment Type of Request Item' inside the FUBR. KBA 2733455 

Set to 'X'

6.Quotations

Parameter ID

What it does / when to use it

Setting / Value

/SCMTMS/FWAQ_TYPE

Sets the default quotation type so users working freight quotation processes do not have to pick it each time.

Freight quotation

7.Optimizer

Parameter ID

What it does / when to use it

Setting / Value

/SCMTMS/EXP

Switches on the optimizer explanation tool, which lets planning analysts understand why the optimizer made a given decision.

Refer : https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blog-posts-by-sap/monday-knowledge-snippet-mks-31-optimizer-explanation-tool/ba-p/12962035

Set to 'X'

/SCMTMS/CS_OPT_DEST

User-defined RFC destination for the Carrier Selection optimizer. Set it when CS optimizer calls must be routed to a specific instance.

Set the target RFC destination when needed

/SCMTMS/VSR_OPT_DEST

Destination for the Vehicle Scheduling and Routing (VSR) optimizer, used to route VSR calls to a specific RFC destination or instance.

Set the target RFC estination when needed

8.Charge Calculation and Conditions

These parameters for condition and tune charge calculation performance and provide explanation and logging

Parameter ID

What it does / when to use it

Setting / Value

/SCMTMS/COND_BUF

Activates the new Condition Buffer by standard

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-members/quick-tip-brf-trace-in-tm-conditions-troubleshooting/ba-p/14035540

Set to ‘X’ or ‘N’

/SCMTMS/COND_EXPL

Activates Condition Explanation Mode.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/wks-condition-explanation-tool/ba-p/13400065

Set to ‘X’

/SCMTMS/TCC_LOG

Switches on the charges log, useful temporarily when investigating freight charge discrepancies.

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blog-posts-by-sap/how-to-enable-logs-in-sap-tm/ba-p/13573418

 

Set to ‘X’

9.Routing and Scheduling

These control rail routing visibility, schedule selection strategy, forced rescheduling and incompatibility defaults

Parameter ID

What it does / when to use it

Setting / Value

/SCMTMS/RAIL_ROUTE

Controls rail routing display: X hides routing without a rate, A displays all routings.

' ' (default value) The system filters out the routing options with a rate equal to zero within the default route

 

Set per preference

/SCMTMS/SCHSTRAT

Defines the strategy for selecting schedules, aligned to your transportation mode or lane planning convention.

Set strategy

/SCMTMS/SCH_FORCE

Forces scheduling after a date change: X forces it everywhere, P limits forced rescheduling to the Transportation Cockpit only.

Set to X or P

/SCMTMS/TSPS_INCSET

User-specific default name for the TSPS incompatibility settings,

Set incompatibility settings

10.Multi-Resource Planning

Parameter ID

What it does / when to use it

Setting / Value

/SCMTMS/MP_RASNOSCH

During reassignment, prevents scheduling of the source freight order. Use it in reassignment scenarios where the source FO should not be rescheduled.

Set to ‘X’

11. Dangerous Goods, Delivery and Geography

Parameter ID

What it does / when to use it

Setting / Value

/SCMTMS/DG_MORE_INFO

Returns more information during the Dangerous Goods check, valuable for compliance and DG specialists.

Set to ‘X’

/SCMTMS/DLV_SHOW_XML

Shows the XML result in the Delivery Proposal report, useful for technical and integration analysts debugging delivery proposals.

Set to ‘X’ for integration analysts

/SCMTMS/GEO_UT_DET

Switches on details of the Geography unit tests, used to validate geography master data.

Use in dev / test environments

12. Authentication, Logging and Messaging

Parameter ID

What it does / when to use it

Setting / Value

/SCMTMS/LDAP_NEW

Controls usage of the new LDAP implementation versus the old one. Set it per the Basis team's LDAP migration status.

Set per Basis LDAP migration status

/SCMTMS/MAX_NUM_LOGS

Maximum number of TM logs retained. Tune it based on storage and performance considerations.

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blog-posts-by-sap/how-to-enable-logs-in-sap-tm/ba-p/13573418

 

Tune for storage / performance

/SCTM/MSG_SAVE

Writes the application log (temporary). Enable it temporarily when you need message or log debugging.

Enable temporarily for debugging

/SCMTMS/USE_INT_ID

Uses internal IDs in message handling, aligned to your integration team's messaging convention.

Set per messaging convention

13. Parallelization and Performance

Parameter ID

What it does / when to use it

Setting / Value

/SCMTMS/PARAPACKSIZE

Number of objects placed in a single package during TM parallelization. Tune with Basis for batch job performance.

Tune with Basis

/SCMTMS/PARAWAITTIME

Queue (wait) time for TM parallelization. Tune with Basis for batch job performance.

Tune with Basis

/SCMTMS/PARAWKP

Number of work processes used in TM parallelization. Tune with Basis based on available system resources.

Tune with Basis on available resources

/SCMTMS/TOR_OPT_BUFF

Uses an optimized buffer for the overview, improving performance on TOR overview screens.

Enable for TOR overview performance

14. Technical, Change Controller and ERP Integration

Parameter ID

What it does / when to use it

Setting / Value

/SCMTMS/UNDO_TEST

Test/dev undo behaviour: N means no undo. Used in test scenarios and not typically set in production.

Test / dev only

/SCMTMS/UUIDCONVEXIT

Activates conversion exits for UUIDs (X = active). Coordinate with Basis or development before changing this.

Coordinate before changing

/SCMTMS/CHACO_ACTIVE

Change Controller behaviour: A is always active, I is inactive for the user. Set I for users who should bypass Change Controller checks, such as batch or technical users. KBA : 2384459 

I for batch / technical users

/SCMTMS/ERP_UNAME

Username of the calling ERP system. Set it on technical or communication users that handle ERP-to-TM integration.

Set on technical / communication users


Best practices and a word of caution

  • Prefer message over dump in production. Set assertion-style parameters such as /SCMTMS/TC_TECASSERT and /SCMTMS/PLN_INCONSIS to the value that sends a message (1) rather than raising a hard assert (0). Keep 0 for dev and test only.
  • Turn logging on temporarily, then off. Switches like /SCMTMS/UI_PLN_LOG, /SCMTMS/TCC_LOG and /SCTM/MSG_SAVE are great for troubleshooting but generate volume. Enable them while investigating an issue and remove them afterwards.
  • Coordinate technical parameters with Basis. The parallelization parameters (/SCMTMS/PARAPACKSIZE, /SCMTMS/PARAWAITTIME, /SCMTMS/PARAWKP) and /SCMTMS/UUIDCONVEXIT affect system behaviour beyond a single user — tune them together with your Basis team.
  • Use parameters for technical users too. Settings such as /SCMTMS/CHACO_ACTIVE = I and /SCMTMS/ERP_UNAME are meant for batch, communication and integration users, not interactive planners.
  • Mind parallel planning. Enabling /SCMTMS/PLN_PARALLEL can speed up a team but also create locking conflicts when planners share documents — introduce it deliberately.
  • Document by role. Maintain a role-to-parameter map so onboarding is consistent and audits are easy.

Conclusion

SAP TM user parameters are an under-appreciated lever. With no custom development, they let you shape the planning experience, control message, route optimizer calls, tune performance and prepare technical users for integration.

The key is to be intentional: enable diagnostics only while you need them, keep production on graceful message handling, and standardise values per role so the experience is predictable across your team.

Thanks for reading, and happy planning!

entry.title: Re: Adding custom field in "Receive Products" app
entry.content:
Dear @KhaingZinTun,

Yes, the warning is relevant. It means that the app uses synchronous views and controller extensions are not supported for such views in SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects. Therefore, any change that depends on a controller extension will be disabled. This is a technical limitation of the app/adaptation scenario, not a setup mistake. The fact that the standard fields under the Information tab and the added fragment are not rendered in the Adaptation Editor is consistent with this limitation and with the editor-preview behavior for this app. In the screenshot, no product appears to be selected in the editor preview. Without a bound record, the Information-tab form has no context to render. The result should therefore be validated after deployment in the application variant, using a product that returns data at runtime

A simple UI flexibility change, such as unhiding or changing an existing field, is not the same as a controller extension. Therefore, it should be tested at runtime even if the Adaptation Editor canvas does not show the expected result

More details below:

SAP KBA 3618851 – synchronous views are detected for this application
SAP Help – Controller Extensions
SAP Help – Extensibility: Look Up Retail Products

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: error transaction INTRASALES- SAP
entry.content:
Business Scenario

T Code VEFU

During Intrastat reporting, we have scenarios where Credit Memo and Debit Memo billing documents need to be linked to the corresponding original invoice for reporting purposes. However, in certain cases, the material/article referenced in the Credit Memo or Debit Memo differs from the material/article in the original invoice.

As a result, the expected linkage between the adjustment document and the original invoice is not established in the Intrastat work file records.

Clarification Required

Could you please confirm:

  1. Does standard SAP ECC provide functionality to manually link a Credit Memo or Debit Memo to an original invoice for Intrastat reporting purposes when the material/article references are different? If yes, is there any standard transaction (e.g., VEFU or any other Foreign Trade / Intrastat transaction) that allows maintenance of the following Intrastat reference fields:

Thanks in advance for any insights.

entry.title: Re: What is Production Planning?
entry.content: Production Planning is the process of determining what to produce, when to produce, how much to produce, and what resources are required to meet customer demand efficiently. It helps manufacturers optimize inventory, machine utilization, workforce allocation, and production schedules while minimizing costs and delays. Modern ERP solutions make production planning more effective by providing real-time visibility into inventory, procurement, and shop-floor operations. Solutions like ZYNO by Elite Mindz, a leading ERP Software Company and ERP Software Provider, help manufacturers streamline production planning, procurement, and resource management through integrated Procurement Software and ERP capabilities.

entry.title: Training about Labor Management in EWM – new collection available
entry.content:
Staying up to date with the latest innovations in warehouse management is essential for organizations aiming to optimize efficiency and remain competitive. That’s why we’re excited to announce an important update to one of our learning offerings: the “Configuring Labor Management in SAP EWM for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition” course.

What’s New?

The newest collection of this course is now available on our learning platform, bringing with it refreshed content aligned with the latest SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition release. This update ensures that learners are equipped with the most current knowledge and best practices in Extended Warehouse Management (EWM).

Spotlight on Innovation: AI-Assisted Predictive Labor Demand Planning

The most exciting addition to the course is the new topic:
“AI-Assisted Predictive Labor Demand Planning.”

This module introduces how artificial intelligence can transform labor management in the warehouse by:

  • Forecasting labor demand more accurately based on historical and real-time data
  • Optimizing workforce allocation to match operational needs
  • Reducing labor costs while maintaining high service levels
  • Improving decision-making through data-driven insights

By leveraging AI, businesses can move beyond reactive planning and adopt a proactive, predictive approach—helping them respond more effectively to fluctuating demand and operational complexities.

Get Started Today

If you’re looking to enhance your planning capabilities and explore how AI can elevate your warehouse operations, this updated course is a great place to start.

entry.title: AI-Assisted Cross-Tier Flexible Constraint Visibility
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's interconnected enterprise landscape, organizations often operate multiple ERP systems across headquarters, regional hubs, and subsidiaries. While each business entity plans independently, supply constraints are frequently managed centrally. This creates a challenge for planners who need visibility into available supply capacity from upstream organizations before making planning decisions.

To address this challenge, we developed an AI-Assisted Cross-Tier Flexible Constraint Visibility solution using Joule. The solution enables production planners in a subsidiary ERP system (Tier 2) to seamlessly query flexible constraints maintained in the headquarters ERP system (Tier 1) using natural language.

By leveraging SAP APIs and Joule's conversational capabilities, planners can instantly understand:

  • Maximum quantity available from headquarters
  • Flexible constraint limits for a material or a Production line
  • Available supply periods with the constraint quantity

This eliminates manual coordination between planning teams and enables faster, data-driven planning decisions.

Business Requirement

Organizations operating in a two-tier ERP landscape often face challenges in coordinating supply and production planning across headquarters which supplies subassemblies and subsidiaries which consumes subassemblies from HQ and manufactures finished product. Headquarters manages production line constraints and material supply constraints of the subassembly which will be considered for Mid-term/long-term planning using pMRP (Predictive Material and Resource Planning) simulation process, while subsidiary planners require timely visibility into these constraints for accurate planning of the finished product. In many cases, obtaining this information involves manual co-ordination, leading to delays, inefficiencies, and potential planning inaccuracies.

To address this challenge, the Joule-powered AI-Assisted Cross-Tier Flexible Constraint Visibility solution provides real-time access to headquarters-managed flexible constraints of subassembly directly within the subsidiary planning environment via joule, enabling more connected and informed planning decisions across

Two-Tier solution with Business Use-case

Consider a global manufacturing company where the headquarters operates on SAP Cloud ERP Private headquartered at European country produces the subassembly compressor and its subsidiary in APJ region operates on SAP Cloud ERP consumes compressor subassembly to manufacture the finished product (let’s say refrigerator). The headquarters maintains flexible constraints of the subassembly that define the maximum quantities available for supply across different planning periods. A production planner in a subsidiary needs to determine how much of a particular material can be supplied from headquarters before finalizing production plan for the finished product. Traditionally, this information would require coordination with headquarters planners, resulting in delays and manual effort.

With AI-Assisted Cross-Tier Flexible Constraint Visibility, the planner at subsidiary can simply ask Joule a natural language query such as,

"What is the maximum quantity headquarters can supply for Material Compressor?”

Joule retrieves the relevant flexible constraint information of the given material from the headquarters system and displays the available quantities with planning periods directly within the subsidiary environment via joule. This enables faster decision-making, improved collaboration between planning teams, and a more connected planning experience across organizational tiers.

Process Flow

Tier 1 – Headquarters (SAP Cloud ERP Private)

  • Supply planners at Tier1 define and maintain Flexible Constraints in SAP Cloud ERP Private.
  • Products or materials are assigned to the relevant flexible constraints.
  • Constraint periods are maintained with available quantities for each planning bucket (weekly, monthly, etc.).

Tier 2 – Subsidiary (SAP Cloud ERP)

  • A production planner at Tier2 initiates a query through Joule.
  • Joule interprets the planner's query and identifies the requested data.
  • Joule retrieves Flexible constraint details, product assignments, and available quantities in real time.
  • Joule consolidates and formats the information into a business-friendly response.
  • The planner at Tier 2 leverages the retrieved supply information in pMRP simulations for mid- and long-term planning of finished products, with the simulation results driving operational planning decisions.

Integration Details

The AI-Assisted Cross-Tier Flexible Constraint Visibility solution leverages standard SAP APIs exposed from the headquarters system (SAP Cloud ERP Private) and orchestrated through Joule. Depending on the planner's request, Joule dynamically invokes the relevant APIs to retrieve flexible constraint information and present it in a business-friendly format within the subsidiary environment.

The integration follows a request-response pattern where Joule acts as the conversational interface, the SAP APIs act as the source of truth for flexible constraint information, and the headquarters ERP system serves as the repository of supply commitments and constraint definitions.

This scenario is triggered when a production planner requests flexible constraint information for a specific material. For example:

"What is the maximum quantity headquarters can supply for Material Compressor in plant 1811 in the next quarter?"

Upon receiving the request, Joule identifies the material and invokes the relevant APIs in the headquarters system. The Flexible Constraint API retrieves the constraint header information, while the Flexible Constraint Period API retrieves the available quantities maintained for each planning period. Joule then consolidates the information and presents the available supply quantities to the planner.

APIs Used

API

Purpose

System

Flexible Constraint

Read flexible constraint header data

SAP Cloud ERP Private

Flexible Constraint Period

Read flexible constraint period and quantity

SAP Cloud ERP Private

Joule Orchestration Logic

Joule serves as the intelligent orchestration layer between the subsidiary planner and the headquarters ERP system. Based on the user's natural language request, Joule automatically invokes the appropriate APIs. The retrieved information is aggregated, interpreted, and presented in a conversational format, eliminating the need for planners to navigate technical applications or manually coordinate with headquarters.

This AI-driven approach provides real-time access to headquarters-managed supply constraints, enabling faster decision-making and more effective collaboration across organizational tiers.

Joule Demo:

To get visibility on the Flexible Constraint Header Data

The screenshot below demonstrates the cross-tier visibility capability, where Flexible Constraint IDs 1402 and 1403, already maintained in the Headquarters system, are retrieved and displayed in real time within the Subsidiary system through Joule.

To get visibility on the flexible constraint quantity for the corresponding period

Business Benefits

  • Real-time cross-tier visibility
  • Faster planning cycles and decision-making
  • Improved supply commitment accuracy
  • Enhanced collaboration across business units
  • AI-driven user experience through Joule

Conclusion

AI-Assisted Cross-Tier Flexible Constraint Visibility demonstrates how Joule can bridge information silos across enterprise planning systems. By enabling production planners in subsidiary organizations to access headquarters constraint information through natural language interactions, organizations can significantly improve planning efficiency and collaboration.

By enabling real-time visibility into headquarters-managed supply constraints, the solution helps create a seamless planning experience where critical supply information is available instantly, regardless of organizational boundaries.

Hope this blog gives you insights on getting cross-tier visibility on flexible constraints using joule.

Happy Learning!! 🙂

entry.title: RERAALCN (ACE for RE Component)
entry.content:
Hi,

is there any way to configure the RERAALCN triggering, simultaneously, two postings? One to accrue the current period and, another to reversal on the 1st day of the next period.

Posting

Date

Description

Accrual Posting

Last day of the current period (e.g., 31.01)

Recognizes the accrued amount in the current period

Reversal Posting

1st day of the next period (e.g., 01.02)

Automatically reverses the accrual to "clear" it before the next run

This approach ensures each period's accrual is self-contained. The reversal on day 1 of the following period neutralizes the prior posting so the next accrual run starts clean.

Thanks and best regards!

entry.title: Re: Material Listing and Exclusion Configuration in S/4HANA.
entry.content: @Alienware One condition type is used for material listing, which allows specific materials to be processed, while the other condition type is used for material exclusion, which restricts specific materials from being processed. Since listing and exclusion serve different business purposes, two separate condition types are maintained in the system. Corresponding condition records are also maintained for each condition type so that the system can clearly identify which materials are permitted through listing and which materials are restricted through exclusion.

entry.title: SAP Business Network will support regulatory consistent processing of e-Invoices in Spain
entry.content:
Europe’s regulatory landscape is shifting, with countries steadily introducing e-invoicing mandates to tackle the VAT gap. An additional country to add to this digital transformation is Spain. 

The Spanish e-invoicing mandate will be rolled out in different phases, starting 12 months after the publication of the ministerial order, which has been announced for October 2026. At that time, companies with an annual turnover exceeding 8 million euros will have to start issuing and receiving invoices, and notify corresponding statuses.  Companies with an annual turnover of less than 8 million euros get a bit more time and only have to start issuing and receiving invoices 24 months after the final publication of the Ministerial Order, and start exchanging corresponding statuses​ 36 months after that final publication. This phased approach aims to improve fiscal transparency and automation while giving organizations time to align their systems and processes. 

The mandate requires businesses to issue and receive domestic electronic B2B invoices compliant with the European standard EN-16931. To ensure a smooth transition, the regulations allow some flexibility in data formatting — supporting CII, UBL, EDIFACT, and Factura-e — as well as in connectivity, enabling suppliers and buyers to exchange data via private platforms, the official public platform or a hybrid combination. Reporting on payment status and acceptance/rejection must be sent to the public platform within 4 days. 

SAP Business Network will support the outbound flow, i.e. issuing invoices via SAP Business Network, as well as the inbound flow to allow buying customers to receive invoices from both the network and external sources. 

In other words,  suppliers on SAP Business Network can keep sending invoices without major changes,  and keep benefiting from the Network value while being fully compliant with the Spanish mandate. With less manual intervention, suppliers are reducing errors and exceptions and allow for business rules validation and faster processing. This leads to quicker approvals and earlier payments.​ 

To support your readiness, we organize two webcasts in the near future on the Spanish e-invoicing mandate and related SAP Business Network support: 

  • English session: July 8, 2026 at 4:00 PM CEST – Register here 
  • Spanish session: July 8, 2026 at 10:00 AM CEST – Register here 

We are looking forward to welcoming you in these short conferences, which will remain available as recordings after the event.

For additional information on e-invoicing compliance in different countries, please visit our SAP Business Network for Suppliers page.

entry.title: Re: Extending Form Templates for Sales Document Output
entry.content:
Great blog post! I'd like to add information about another enhancement that became available in release 2602.2 — the ability to use calculated fields in custom form templates for sales document output.

With this new capability, key users can now define calculated header or item fields (along with their BAdI implementations) for sales order output. When generating the output, the system calculates the values of these fields at runtime based on the respective BAdI logic. This effectively extends the Form Data Provider (FDP) service for order confirmation (FDP_V1_ORDER_CONFIRM_SRV).

Typical Use Cases:

Here are some realistic business scenarios where calculated fields shine:

  1. Dynamic Delivery Lead Time — Calculate and print the expected delivery lead time on the order confirmation based on the requested delivery date, plant calendar, and current workload — giving customers a reliable commitment date.
  2. Customer Tier / Loyalty Status Label — Derive a customer's loyalty tier (e.g., "Gold Partner", "Preferred Customer") from their purchase history or customer classification, and print it prominently on the confirmation for a personalized touch.
  3. Computed Total Net Weight or Volume — Aggregate net weight or total volume across all line items at runtime, useful when the standard output form does not carry a pre-calculated total, or when custom UoM conversions are involved.
  4. Payment Terms Description — Combine payment term code, cash discount percentage, and due date logic into a human-readable description (e.g., "2% 10 days, net 30") derived dynamically from multiple fields and conditions.

How it works:

  1. In the Custom Fields app, create a data source extension for the Sales: Sales Order Confirmation Form data source.
  2. On the Calculated Fields tab, create your calculated fields — header-level fields for SalesOrderHeaderExtensionType, and item-level fields for SalesOrderItemExtensionType.
  3. Enter a description for the corresponding BAdI implementation, then save and publish the extension.
  4. Open the BAdI implementation and add custom logic — the header-level BAdI is SD_SLS_FDP_ORDR_HDR_CALC and the item-level BAdI is SD_SLS_FDP_ORDR_ITM_CALC.

In your custom logic, you can determine the field values by applying hardcoded values, retrieving data from CDS views, or calling classes/methods — making this a very flexible mechanism.

Example BAdI Implementation (SD_SLS_FDP_ORDR_ITM_CALC):

Below is a short pseudo-code snippet illustrating how you might implement the item-level BAdI to calculate a "Confirmed Delivery Days" field:

METHOD if_sd_sls_fdp_ordr_itm_calc~calculate.
  LOOP AT it_sales_order_item INTO DATA(item).
    " Calculate delivery days from today to requested delivery date
    DATA(delivery_date) = item-RequestedDeliveryDate.
    DATA(today)         = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ).
    DATA(delivery_days) = delivery_date - today.

    " Populate the calculated extension field
    DATA(extension) = VALUE salesorderitemextensiontype(
      salesorderitem            = item-SalesOrderItem
      yy1_confirmeddelivdays_it = |{ delivery_days }| ).

    INSERT extension INTO TABLE ct_sales_order_item_extension.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

This pattern — reading standard item data, applying business logic, and writing results into the extension type — applies equally to header-level calculations via SD_SLS_FDP_ORDR_HDR_CALC.

Note: The BAdIs only appear in the Custom Logic app after the relevant data source extension has been published in the Custom Fields app.

For more details, see: How to Extend a Custom Form Template with Calculated Fields

entry.title: @sap/cds-rfc: RFC_COMMUNICATION_FAILURE (NIECONN_BROKEN) in BAS using cds bind --exec, but works aft
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am trying to run a CAP Node.js application locally in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) using hybrid mode (cds bind --exec). The application connects to an on-premise SAP system via Destination Service + Connectivity Service + Cloud Connector using @SAP/cds-rfc.

The exact same application works perfectly when deployed to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry, but fails when run locally in BAS.

Environment

  • SAP Business Application Studio

  • Node.js: 22.13.1

  • @SAP/cds: 9.9.1

  • @SAP/cds-dk: 9.9.1

  • @SAP/cds-rfc: 2.2.1

  • @SAP-rfc/node-rfc-library: 1.1.2

  • @SAP-cloud-sdk/connectivity: 4.7.0

Hybrid setup

Service bindings are created successfully:

cds bind --to navisaccess-auth
cds bind --to navisaccess-connectivity
cds bind --to navisaccess-destination

Running:

cds bind --exec -- node rfctest.js

correctly injects all Cloud Foundry service bindings.

Minimal reproducible example

const cds = require("@sap/cds");

(async () => {
    try {
        console.log("Connecting...");

        const client = await cds.connect.to("P23_ABAP", {
            credentials: {
                destination: "P23_ABAP"
            }
        });

        console.log("Connected.");

        console.log("Calling RFC_PING...");

        const result = await client.send("RFC_PING", {});

        console.log(result);
    } catch (err) {
        console.error(err);
    }
})();

Output

Connecting...
Connected.
Calling RFC_PING...

RFCException NIECONN_BROKEN :
unable to receive data from connectivityproxy.internal.cf.ap10.hana.ondemand.com:20001

RFC_COMMUNICATION_FAILURE

Stack trace points to:

RFCService.begin()
createBapiClientConnectionHandler()
RFCClientAddonConnectionHandler.open()

What I have verified

  • cds bind works successfully.

  • Destination Service binding is successful.

  • Connectivity Service binding is successful.

  • XSUAA binding is successful.

  • cds.connect.to() succeeds.

  • The destination is resolved successfully.

  • The RFC client is created successfully.

  • The failure occurs only when the first RFC call (RFC_PING or GET_SYSTEM_TIME_REMOTE) is executed.

  • The exact same code works after deploying the application to Cloud Foundry using cf push.

  • It has has never been tested locally in BAS.

My question

Is RFC access through @SAP/cds-rfc + SAP Connectivity Service + Cloud Connector officially supported when running locally using cds bind --exec (hybrid mode)?

If yes, is there any additional configuration required for BAS or hybrid execution?

Or is RFC communication through Cloud Connector only supported for applications running inside the Cloud Foundry runtime?

Any guidance or documentation would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: SuccessFactors Expert Community Meeting October 28 in Copenhagen
entry.content:
If you are a SAP SuccessFactors customer based in Denmark or southern Sweden, join the Pentos Expert Community Meeting Nordics series. We will spend a few focused hours on knowledge sharing, key 2H 2026 release news, and highlights from SuccessConnect - with practical perspectives for HR and HR IT teams.

https://www.pentos.com/insights/events-and-webinars/upcoming-events/expert-community-meeting-nordics

Pentos is co-hosting this together with SAP in the Copenhagen offices; signup is required and knowledge is guaranteed.

entry.title: Re: Characteristics-Based Dispatching in SAP Digital Manufacturing Resource Orchestration
entry.content:
Hi @Peter_Steenbakkers ,

Sure! You can customize the <CustomFieldList> section of LOIPRO Workflow XSLT as described in the blog post above to map any available IDOC field, to make it available in Manage Orders > Custom Data.

To display order custom data in PODs for the Workers, you can evaluate custom plugins. I drafted one a while back, you can take a look at: https://github.com/manoelfranklins/POD2.0-orderConfigurationData

Hope it helps! Br, Manoel

Business Rule Limit

error message: 
Failed

You can deploy 200 business rules at most to the same plant.
Running multiple business rules with overlapping conditions of the same business service and trigger point can cause performance issues. You can activate the business rule you selected while the conflicting one will be deactivated accordingly." 

mention inside sap dm documentation. https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/production-process-designer/manage-business-rules?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Managing Invoices with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management (now SAP Ariba Invoicing)
entry.content:
Hello @Abhijit_Shinde,

We are implementing SAP CIM (Ariba Invoicing) for our customer and they want to use only manual, email and peppol via SAP DRC as inbound channel. 
Countries in scope are: Germany (XRechnung requirement) and Sweden (PEPPOL requirement).

1. Does SAP Business Network comes together with SAP Ariba Invoicing (CIM) or does it need separate license ? If no, PO needs to be sent from S/4 Hana public cloud to SBN, this would be commerce automation. would be extra cost for this setup ?
From our perspective SBN would be best option to create and submit Xrechnung for germany. 

2. If supplier creates X rechnung from SBN, do we still need SAP DRC to receive and fetch invoice in CIM ?

3. Does receiving invoicing from PEPPOL Network (via DRC) to CIM still need SBN Integration ? If yes, what will be the flow ? If using PEPPOL network, do suppliers need onboarding on PEPPOL just like SBN?

4. If supplier sends XML (Xrechnung) via email, do we need SAP DRC to extract that data into CIM? If yes, how it can be fetched, because in this case we need SAP DRC and RPA both.

5. Our customer has entilements for sap ariba invoicing and sap ariba invoicing inbound processing module but they are yet to provision it. will be provisioned new way (sap managed) or old way (customer managed). How to know for which scope entitlements are (4N6 or 7YH)?

 

Thanks for your response.

entry.title: Unable to select transaction from transaction history dropdown in WEB GUI
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

We are experiencing an issue with the transaction history dropdown in SAP GUI.

When clicking on the transaction field (e.g. entering SM51) and opening the list of recently used transactions, the entries are displayed correctly. However, clicking directly on the transaction name does not select it.

The transaction can only be selected by clicking on the far left edge of the list item. Clicking on the text or anywhere else within the row has no effect.

This behavior makes the transaction history difficult to use and appears to indicate that the clickable area is misaligned with the displayed text.

We would like to integrate this functionality directly from the SAP backend (ABAP) using the odata service - FML_UPLMATINVPR_SRV without using the Fiori UI. Specifically, our requirement is:

Read an existing Excel file stored within SAP (e.g., MIME repository / application server)
Pass the file content programmatically to the OData service
Trigger the same processing flow (upload → validation → batch → post)

The Excel file is not uploaded via Fiori UI
Instead, the file content (XSTRING) is read directly from SAP repository
And sent programmatically via HTTP client (CL_HTTP_CLIENT) or similar framework?

Thanks & Regards

Ritushree

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 19 - CDS source formatter
Function module RSDMD_API_DELETE does not delete
entry.content:
Hello BI fans,

we use FM RSDMD_API_DELETE in a custom webdynpro to delete a single value of an infoobject. Of course the value is not used in any info provider (no transaction data).

The RC is always 0, but the value is not deleted.

CALL FUNCTION 'RSDMD_API_DELETE'
            EXPORTING
              i_iobjnm               = ‘HH_XX’

              i_t_data                 = lt_attr_neu

              i_flg_delete_all     = rs_c_true

              i_flg_delete_texts = rs_c_false.

LT_ATTR_NEU[1] (in the debugger)

RECORD_NO                                              1

IOBJNM                                            HH_XX

VALUE                                             value

A) If HH_XX is a compounded infoobject the VALUE field is concatenated (1st compound field, 2nd compound field, value).

-> RC is always 0, but nothing is deleted

B) If HH_XX is a non compounded infoobject the VALUE field is only the value.

-> RC is always 0, but nothing is deleted.

I have also tried with COMMIT WORK after CALL FUNCTION and RC=0, but it makes no difference.

And the worst thing: when I pass a non-existent value to the FM the RC is 0 too! Crazy!

Unfortunately the FM has no exceptions and the documentation doesn’t explain the meaning of the export parameters.

The 4 export parameters in the debugger of the FM RSDMD_API_DELETE (before ENDFUNCTION):

L_RESULT                                       6

L_T_SIDVAL_USED                        [0x2(12)]Initial Standard Table

E_COUNT_USE                               0

L_T_PROTOCO                                [0x13(654)]Initial Standard Table

The included FM RSDMD_DEL_MASTER_DATA explains the export parameter e_result in his documentation (these should be constants):

RSDMD_C_ALL_DELETED                      0

RSDMD_C_PARTS_DELETED                  1

RSDMD_C_BG_STARTED                         2

RSDMD_C_NONE_DELETED                  4

RSDMD_C_NONE_DELETEABLE          

RSDMD_C_ERROR                                    5

But what means the return value 6?

If I don't pass the paramter i_flg_delete_texts L_RESULT=0, but nothin has been deleted.

 

Does anyone have experience using this FM? I don’t find a note according this problem.

The release is SAP BW/4HANA 2023, DW4CORE 4000006

 

Thanks and best wishes, Britta

entry.title: Re: Behind the Curtains - what's new about Maintenance Certificates
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

my question goes in the direction of 1jules question and, apologies, I don't understand your answer.

We are struggling in setup of maintenance certificate check in combination with Cloud ALM.

We have configured Cloud ALM option for additional Hana metrics (https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/setup-managed-services/calm-setup-hana.html)

Federation Lead is always saphostagent of HANA DB
Federation peer are always the SAP application server of the HANA DB

Cloud ALM monitoring is working by that.

Now we want to configure maintenance certificate check, like described here.

We have configured SAP Support Backbone Proxy on a central saphostagent where saprouter SRO is running (sap router for SAP to access the systems). Connection to SAP is working. We could already added saphostagents to the federated network and maintenance certificate checks in SAP systems works. So for this configuration the setup is:

Federation Lead is always saphostagent on SRO
Federation peer are always the SAP application server

When we now want to add SAP application server to SRO where we have already configured Cloud ALM we got error message:

Peer [FQDN hostname SAP AppServer] is already part of another SAP Host Agent Federation [Lead CA: 'CN=FQDN hostname HANA DB, OU=SAPHostAgent.INTCOM, OU=SAP Host Agent Certificate Authority, O=SAP Host Agent, C=DE'].

So a federation peer can only be connected to one federation lead.

Question is, in this scenario where we want to use Cloud ALM and maintenance certificate checks via federated network what would be SAP recommendation for set up, especially in terms of who should be federation lead and who federation peer?
Would the setup one federation lead (SRO) and all other saphostagents (SAP application server, HANA DB) are federation peers connects to SRO works for Cloud ALM as well?

Best regards,
Dennis

entry.title: Re: "System temporarily unavailable" for certification C_CPI_2601
entry.content: I've encountered the same "System temporarily unavailable" error on multiple scheduled attempts for C_CPI_2601. On the occasion I was able to access the exam, I observed a separate issue in Task 2: the on-premise API Provider connection test returned HTTP 503, and the publish-to-Developer-Hub step was not available. In my case the affected attempt was regranted automatically, so no attempt appears to have been deducted. I've raised support cases covering both the access error and the in-exam environment issue: 5000353119 — Issue accessing C_CPI_2601 exam environment (raised 2026-05-25) 5000354127 — Repeated issue accessing exam environment (raised 2026-05-27) 5000372265 — Task 2 cannot be completed: provider 503 + Developer Hub not provisioned (raised 2026-06-22, in process) Responses so far have referenced maintenance and known issues being addressed, but I haven't yet received a confirmed resolution. I'll share an update here if support provides further detail. It may be helpful for others seeing the same behaviour to note their dates so the pattern is easier to track.

entry.title: Can we anywhere see when SAP change the rules in SAP Docuent AI?
entry.content:
Can we see if and when SAP changed the LLM or other rules in the OCR part of SAP Document AI.

We use SAP Document AI via API calls and today we have had a number of requests that timed out. When we look in the UI, the documents are "DONE", but the API call did not answer within 1 minute. The response is usually much faster and we have never seen this problem before. Also when we re-load the documents the response time is about 10-20 seconds. 

The reason I suspect some updates is that after some time, the timeout stopped, but now the data is suddenly OCR extracted in an other way. Before we had an line item OCR error - we had same error since we starte the project a few months ago. Now this error  is solved and instead we see a new error.
Best Regards

Thomas Madsen Nielsen

entry.title: Re: SAC Input Control issue
entry.content:
Hi @MangeshC 
Have you checked the following? 

  1. Open the input control filter dialog for Company Code
  2. Click Settings
  3. Find the Available Members setting
  4. Change it from the default (which tries to fetch from master data independently) to "Members in Result Set"

If this does not work, you could raise an OSS ticket to component CA-GTF-ANA-CNT-BPP directly. You can check here 3740331 - How to request support for content packages? - SAP for Me how to do it. However, I can imagine you need a quicker solution, so then the best workaround would be to create a Scripted dropdown button.

entry.title: Using SSO with Hana 2.0 and Azure AD
entry.content:
We have SAP Hana DB 2.00.079 and i want to login XS Classic engine with Azure AD. I created a Azure test account, configure single sign-on with SAML and everything working as expected.

I changed Hana side config/certificates for Azure prod but I couldn't login with prod Azure account. I enabled the traces and I can see below error.
Authentication SAMLAuthenticator.cpp(01122) : %%% [error: 4040007] [correlation id: 0x9e9a3ab65af736fe] Internal error occurred: Invalid principal id for principal .
XSSession XSSessionLifecycle.cpp(00379) : Assertion authentication for user xxx@xxx.com failed with reason: ptime::PtimeException

I am login Azure test account with user/pass. Prod Azure account working with windows session, so it is using X509 login to Azure.

I checked both Prod and Test Azure SAML Responses, key differences like below.

ProdTest
<Subject>
<NameID Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress">xxx@xxx.com</NameID>
<Subject>
<NameID Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:unspecified">xxx@xxx.com</NameID>

<AttributeStatement>

<Attribute Name="http://schemas.microsoft.com/claims/authnmethodsreferences">
<AttributeValue>http://schemas.microsoft.com/ws/2008/06/identity/authenticationmethod/x509</AttributeValue>
<AttributeValue>http://schemas.microsoft.com/claims/multipleauthn</AttributeValue>
</Attribute>

<AttributeStatement>

<Attribute Name="http://schemas.microsoft.com/claims/authnmethodsreferences">
<AttributeValue>http://schemas.microsoft.com/ws/2008/06/identity/authenticationmethod/password</AttributeValue>
<AttributeValue>http://schemas.microsoft.com/claims/multipleauthn</AttributeValue>
<AttributeValue>http://schemas.microsoft.com/ws/2008/06/identity/authenticationmethod/unspecified</AttributeValue>
</Attribute>
<AuthnStatement AuthnInstant="2026-06-23T06:58:06.566Z" SessionIndex="_ef3f88b7-4876-40c6-b948-0c838ab31c00">
<AuthnContext>
<AuthnContextClassRef>urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:ac:classes:Unspecified</AuthnContextClassRef>
</AuthnContext>
</AuthnStatement>
<AuthnStatement AuthnInstant="2026-06-19T08:47:16.019Z" SessionIndex="_b98a9868-70a8-4137-a071-eef985d76100">
<AuthnContext>
<AuthnContextClassRef>urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:ac:classes:Password</AuthnContextClassRef>
</AuthnContext>
</AuthnStatement>

Hana can read to user NameID from SAML Response but i couldn't match the user. I hope someone can share some clue.
